<!-- DO NOT EDIT — provenance artifact; regenerate via the workflow, not by hand -->

Both source claims are confirmed: topology is closed `{Pooled, Resident}` (no Bounded), and `ResourcePhase` is lifecycle-only — no `Warming`/`Recovering`/`Tainted`/`Saturated`, no load surface, no admission read. The adversarial critique's grounding holds. Now writing the matrix.

---

# CASE-FIT MATRIX — Resource Topology Model

Verdict legend: **FITS** = closed `{Pooled, Resident, Bounded}` + acquire/release covers it · **FITS+ADD(x)** = fits once contract addition x lands · **DOESN'T-FIT** = breaks acquire→guard→release framing structurally.

Topology vocabulary: `Pooled` (N interchangeable, checkout/recycle), `Resident` (one shared, clone-on-acquire), `Bounded` (restored: `Capped(runtime N)` / `Exclusive` / `Unbounded`-token-gated). CUSTOM = author-named topology the closed set can't express.

| # | Resource | Topology | Availability signal (phase / load / health) | Scope + dedup key | VERDICT |
|---|----------|----------|----------------------------------------------|-------------------|---------|
| 1 | **Postgres pool** (sqlx/deadpool) | **Pooled**, runtime cap | phase Ready/Saturated; load `(open,idle,in_use,max,waiters)`+est_wait; health `SELECT 1` cheap | Tenant; `DSN+pool-fp+SlotIdentity` | **FITS+ADD(post-checkout session-init, graded-load surface, Saturated phase)** — `after_connect`/`SET` leaks across actions without per-acquire init hook |
| 2 | **reqwest client** (shared) | **Resident** | phase Ready-by-construction; load **none** (hyper pool not introspectable); health optional HEAD | Org/Tenant; `base_url+headers+TLS/proxy-fp` | **FITS+ADD(load-introspection must be OPTIONAL/None)** — degenerate case; forcing open/max would be a lie |
| 3 | **Telegram bot** (shared-singleton, rate-limited) | **Resident** + external limiter | phase Ready/Saturated(rate budget spent); load = **rate-remaining/Retry-After from headers** (limiter-owned, not topology); health `getMe` (consumes quota) | Org/Tenant; `api-base+bot-token-fp` | **FITS+ADD(phase driven by external resilience limiter; not a Bounded permit)** — temporal budget refills on a clock, not on release |
| 4 | **Kafka consumer-client** | **CUSTOM `ExclusiveStatefulOwner`** | phase Ready/Recovering(rebalancing)/Tainted; load = **consumer lag + rebalance flag**; health assignment-non-empty | Execution/Action, **never shared**; `group.id+topics+member-fp` | **DOESN'T-FIT** — streaming handle pinned to one trigger for hours; `release`=trigger-teardown; engine never multiplexes steps onto it; `Resident`(many sharers) can't express single-stateful-owner |
| 5 | **Rate+token-limited LLM client** | **Resident** + external limiter | phase Ready/Saturated/Recovering(429 backoff)/Tainted(401); load = **req+token-remaining from headers**; health `GET /models` | Org/Tenant; `base_url+model-defaults+tier-fp`; **cred NOT in key** | **FITS+ADD(externally-fed phase; token-budget in resilience not topology)** — two orthogonal limiters (req/min + token/min); closed-vs-open is irrelevant, layering is the question |
| 6 | **Stateful AI agent session** | **CUSTOM `KeyedExclusivePassivated`** | phase per-id Ready/InUse/Evicted; aggregate live-sessions/max+mem; health is-resident-not-evicted | Execution/Workflow; **session-id (intrinsic identity), NOT config-fp** | **DOESN'T-FIT** — acquire is reattach-by-identity not checkout; session must **survive release across multi-day wait** with durable identity; biggest model gap |
| 7 | **FFmpeg process pool** | **Bounded `Capped(runtime N=num_cpus)`** | phase Ready/Saturated; load permits_used/total (est_wait near-useless, 3-orders variance → binary admit/defer); health `-version`+free-disk | Tenant/Org (the **semaphore** is the instance); `binary-path+hwaccel-fp` | **FITS+ADD(null/permit-only Runtime — acquire leases a permit, not a client object)** — process spawned in action body, argv is per-call |
| 8 | **Headless browser N-pages** | **CUSTOM `Multiplexed`** (or M-browsers×N-pages composite) | phase Ready/Warming(cold-start)/Recovering; load open_pages/max+**browser RSS**; health CDP round-trip (not socket liveness) | Workflow/Tenant, **never cross-tenant**; `launch-args-fp+tenant` | **FITS+ADD(per-acquire session-init=open-Page, fallible async release=close-Page, Warming phase)**; **DOESN'T-FIT** the M×N composite — no flat variant expresses "pool of multiplexers" |
| 9 | **gRPC N-streams** (HTTP/2 channel) | **Resident** (cap peer-negotiated) | phase from `connectivity_state`→Ready/Warming/Recovering/Tainted; load active-streams/**MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS** (mutates from peer); health gRPC Health/Check (wire RPC) | Org/Tenant; `authority+TLS-identity+channel-opts-fp` | **FITS+ADD(load must PULL from live resource — cap is peer-mutable, not stored const; acquire-granularity≠concurrency-granularity)** |
| 10 | **SSH connection→sessions** | **CUSTOM `Multiplexed`** + exec/SFTP sub-resources | phase Ready/Saturated(MaxSessions)/Tainted; load open-channels/MaxSessions; health keepalive global-request | Tenant/Workflow; `host:port+user+auth-fp+known-host` | **FITS+ADD(two acquire shapes/subsystem selector; Saturated-defer≠Tainted-recover; host-key-change=refusable recovery)** — N-channels-over-1-transport is none of Pooled/Resident/Bounded |
| 11 | **Serial-port exclusive** | **Bounded `Exclusive` (N=1)** | phase Ready/Saturated(busy)/Tainted(unplugged); load busy-bit+holder+held-duration; health benign probe (`AT`/`*IDN?`) | Tenant/Org; **stable hardware-id (USB serial#/VID:PID), NOT `/dev/ttyUSB0`** (path renumbers) | **FITS+ADD(ordered reset-on-release barrier before re-issue; poison-on-panic; dedup key=hardware-id not config-fp)** |
| 12 | **Distributed lock + TTL renew** | **CUSTOM `RevocableLease`** | phase Ready/Saturated(held-elsewhere)/Tainted(store-unreachable→may-have-lost-it); load held/free+**TTL-remaining+renewer-healthy**; health verify-fencing-token-still-mine | Workflow/Execution; `store+lock-name+fencing-token`; **dedup-by-config is a CORRECTNESS BUG** (rivals, not shared) | **DOESN'T-FIT** — needs **renew-while-held heartbeat** (no hook between acquire/release), **fencing token on guard**, guard **third-party-revocable mid-use**; can't hold TTL across 3-day wait → yield+reacquire |
| 13 | **Resource across 3-day durable wait** | **CUSTOM (durability axis over any base)** | at resume re-check **as if acquiring fresh**; load must include TTL-vs-wall-clock; health runs **detached from any worker** | Execution; **reattach-token persisted in journal** (not in-mem `Arc`) | **DOESN'T-FIT** — in-mem `Runtime`/`Box<dyn Any>` guard can't survive process restart; needs **release-on-park + reacquire-on-resume by durable token**; belongs partly in engine/storage tier |
| 14 | **Redis multiplexed** | **Resident** | phase Ready/Recovering; load **inflight-pipeline-depth+RTT** (not open/max); health PING | Tenant/Workflow; `node-urls+db+TLS+auth-fp` | **FITS+ADD(generic-metric load — inflight depth, not pool occupancy)** — don't model as Pooled by default |
| 15 | **Redis Streams consumer-group** | **CUSTOM batch-lease-with-ACK** | phase Ready; load **PEL-depth+stream-lag**; health `XINFO STREAM` | Tenant/Workflow; `stream+group+url` | **FITS+ADD(semantic release — ACK-on-success/return-to-PEL-on-crash; lag as load)** — richer than drop-to-release but tolerates lease via durable redelivery |
| 16 | **Redis Pub/Sub subscriber** | **CUSTOM streaming-handle** (or engine-daemon) | phase subscribed/connected; load **dropped-msg/lag** (lossy); health socket liveness | Workflow/Execution; `url+channel-set` | **DOESN'T-FIT** — acquire a *stream you drain*, not a value you return; release=UNSUBSCRIBE; arguably leaves nebula-resource for engine-daemon land |
| 17 | **WASM/Python sandbox instance** | **CUSTOM `Ephemeral/Disposable`** (+ Resident module-cache) | phase live/max+warm-module-ready(Warming-while-compiling); load **fuel/memory-budget remaining**; health instantiate-trivial smoke | instance=Execution/Action, cache=Tenant; instance **never deduped**, cache=`code-hash+policy-fp` | **FITS+ADD(fresh-per-acquire, no-reuse, no-share, discard-on-release)** — `Pooled` reuses, `Resident` shares, `Exclusive` reuses-serially; none express "disposable" (security: state contamination = hole) |
| 18 | **Kafka producer (shared)** | **Resident** | phase Ready/Tainted; load **librdkafka queue-depth/buffer-max** (author-supplied gauge); health metadata refresh | Org/Tenant; `bootstrap+SASL-fp+client-cfg-hash` | **FITS+ADD(graceful async destroy with deadline — flush ordered before drop; author-supplied load gauge)** |
| 19 | **AMQP connection→channels** | **Pooled (channels)** runtime cap=channel-max ⊕ Resident(conn) | phase Ready/Recovering; load channels-open/channel-max+unacked/prefetch; health AMQP heartbeat | conn=Tenant/Workflow, channel=Execution; `AMQP-URI(vhost+host+cred-fp)` | **FITS+ADD(parent-generation invalidation — conn death atomically invalidates channel pool; session-init-on-checkout=channel-open with network I/O)** |
| 20 | **CDC replication slot / WS market-data stream** | **CUSTOM long-lived consume-handle** | phase stream-alive+last-event-age; load **lag (LSN/consumer-lag), not permits**; health heartbeat-events-present | Execution/Workflow; `endpoint+slot-name`; **dedup risky** (one active reader) | **DOESN'T-FIT** — streaming handle held for workflow life; admission is **lag/liveness not permits**; cardinality is per-workflow not per-step; owned resumption cursor must survive reconnect+suspend |
| 21 | **DynamoDB / cloud SDK (STS creds)** | **Resident** + external limiter | phase Ready/Tainted(creds-expired)/Recovering; load **none local** — `ProvisionedThroughputExceeded`/RCU-WCU headroom (remote); health `DescribeTable`+STS-expiry-timer | Org/Tenant; `region+role-ARN+STS-SlotIdentity` (time-expiring) | **FITS+ADD(reactive-only cred re-mint for 1.0 — ADR-0084 defers proactive to 1.1; step may hit expired creds and must retry)** |
| 22 | **Floating license / GPU device** | **Bounded `Capped(runtime K)`** (license) / `Exclusive`(device, N=hardware-count) | phase Ready/Saturated/Warming(model-load); load **seats-used/total** or **VRAM-used/total**; health license-server-query/NVML | Org; `license-server+feature` / `device-UUID` | **FITS+ADD(dynamic cap — license can shrink mid-run, MIG slices reconfigure; renew-heartbeat on held seat)** — cleanest proof cap must be runtime-set-and-queryable, not const generic |

# CONTRACT ADDITIONS THE MATRIX FORCED

Ranked by case-count (how many of the 22 demand it). This is the must-have-1.0 vs defer-1.1 split.

| # | Contract addition | Cases hitting it | Count | Verdict |
|---|-------------------|------------------|-------|---------|
| **A1** | **Restored `Bounded`** with **runtime-config cap** (`Capped(N)`/`Exclusive`/`Unbounded`), N validated as typed Error not const-generic/panic | 7,11,12(excl),17,22 + permit-governors | **5+** | **MUST-1.0.** Unanimous across every domain and even the adversary. The one real gap. Mirror `PoolRuntime::try_new` fail-closed. |
| **A2** | **Admission phase axis** (`Warming`/`Saturated`/`Tainted`/`Recovering`) **separate from** lifecycle `ResourcePhase` — `ResourcePhase` is lifecycle, not admission | 1,3,5,7,8,9,10,11,12,17,21,22 | **12** | **MUST-1.0 (split: keep Warming/Recovering/Tainted; CONTEST Saturated).** Adversary's strongest counter: `Saturated` is load-dressed-as-phase and TOCTOU-prone. See A3. |
| **A3** | **Load introspection** — **OPTIONAL**, **author-supplied generic snapshot** (not fixed `(open,max)`), sync `Copy`, **eventually-consistent/advisory** | 1,4,7,8,9,10,14,15,18,19,20,22 (metric shape varies every time) | **12** | **MUST-1.0 as DIAGNOSTICS-ONLY.** The hard tension: domains want it for *gating*; adversary proves `stats()` is non-atomic so any pre-acquire gate is TOCTOU-by-construction. **Resolution: admission = try-acquire-and-park on the existing error taxonomy (`Backpressure`/`Exhausted{retry_after}`), NOT read-and-decide.** Load surface stays `Option`, never feeds the scheduler. This satisfies both camps. |
| **A4** | **Per-acquire session-init hook** (post-checkout init + pre-release reset), distinct from create/destroy | 1,6,8,10,17,19,20 + AMQP/WS subscribe | **7** | **MUST-1.0.** Correctness bug, not nicety — session state (`SET`/PRAGMA/cwd/txn) leaks across actions/tenants without it. |
| **A5** | **Fallible async `release`/reset with ordering** (reset-before-re-issue; reset can fail→evict; poison-on-panic) — not `Drop` glue | 1(Pooled reset),8,11,16,17,19,20 + Exclusive | **7** | **MUST-1.0.** Applies to **Pooled too** (dirty conn re-pooled), not just Exclusive. Collides head-on with canon §11.4 "async release best-effort on crash" — for WS/AMQP a missed teardown leaks server-side state/quota. |
| **A6** | **Graceful async `destroy(timeout)`** ordered before drop (flush/drain/close) | 1,4,8,18(flush),19,20 + NATS drain + stdio reap | **6+** | **MUST-1.0.** `Drop` is insufficient — in-flight batches/streams lost; cost/safety leaks (GPU/VM). |
| **A7** | **Renew-while-held heartbeat + fencing token on guard** (mid-hold revocation reaches the running step) | 12,15(re-claim),22 + leased-VM/lock family | **3–6** | **MUST-1.0 for the lock/lease family; DEFER the generic case to 1.1.** A held lease is not passive; this is genuinely outside acquire→guard→release. Adversary cuts it as "no consumer" — but distributed-lock-with-TTL is an enumerated must-have case (#12). Scope to `RevocableLease` topology only. |
| **A8** | **Suspend/passivation: release-on-park + reacquire-on-resume by durable reattach-token** (guard ≠ in-mem `Box<dyn Any>`) | 6,12,13,20 + any lease across wait | **4** | **DEFER-1.1 from nebula-resource; the engine/storage tier owns it.** This is the **single biggest structural break** but the adversary is right that durability belongs to engine/journal, not the topology trait. 1.0 contract must only **not forbid** a detachable guard + expose a reattach key. Trigger.dev's onWait/onResume + DBOS config_name registry are the prior-art templates. |
| **A9** | **Streaming handle** (acquire yields a stream you drain for the action's life; release=teardown; admission=lag/liveness not permits) | 4,16,20 (+15 batch variant, 8 page-stream) | **3–5** | **DEFER-1.1, possibly OUT-OF-SCOPE.** #4/#16/#20 arguably belong in engine-daemon land (the retired EventSource/Daemon shape), not nebula-resource. 1.0 must only not forbid a guard outliving one call. |
| **A10** | **Dedup-key corrections**: (a) worker-node for local resources, (b) stable-hardware-id surviving path churn, (c) **anti-dedup / affinity-key** (same config ⇒ must-NOT-share for stateful sessions; never-share for disposables), (d) cluster/store-authoritative for locks | 6,11,12,16(SQLite),17 + agent-session | **5** | **MUST-1.0.** The `(key,scope,SlotIdentity)+fingerprint` dedup assumes "same config ⇒ share." Stateful/disposable/lock resources **invert** this. Needs an explicit share/affinity/anti-dedup mode. Adversary's §3 warns the inverse: author caches below SlotIdentity re-open cross-tenant bleed — so this must be framework-enforced, not author-discretion. |
| **A11** | **Cost-aware active `check()`** (cheap `SELECT 1` vs costly `PRAGMA quick_check`/`DescribeTable`; per-stream/round-trip/sentinel/throughput, not socket-liveness) | 1,6,7,8,11,12,20 + every multiplexed/streaming shape | **7** | **MUST-1.0.** Socket-liveness is a false-green for every multiplexed/streaming resource. Needs a check-cost class so the engine schedules expensive probes less often. K8s three-probe model (liveness/readiness/startup, fail-closed) is the template. |
| **A12** | **Parent-generation recovery** (two-level: conn death atomically invalidates channel pool; recovery re-runs author init, not transport-reconnect) | 8,17,19,20 + MCP respawn | **4** | **MUST-1.0 for AMQP/browser families.** `BrokenCheck`/recovery-gate must key on parent-resource generation, not per-instance health. |
| **A13** | **Resource-owned supervised background task** (MQTT EventLoop, WS read-pump) with teardown ordering (abort after drain, before close) | MQTT,WS(8,20) | **2** | **DEFER-1.1.** Fits-by-convention today (stuff `JoinHandle` in `Runtime`, abort in `shutdown`). Deserves a first-class contract eventually; low case-count. |
| **A14** | **Open topology trait** (author-implementable, beyond closed set) | 4,6,8(M×N),10,16,17,20 | **6–7** | **STRONGLY CONTESTED — recommend KEEP CLOSED for 1.0.** See resolution below. |

---

# THE OPEN-VS-CLOSED DECISION — where the evidence genuinely splits

Six domains independently argue **FOR open** (multiplexed-over-one #9/#10/#19, M×N composite #8, disposable/anti-dedup #17, streaming #4/#16/#20, two-models-one-resource #4-DB). The adversarial critique argues **decisively FOR closed**, and its grounding is the strongest single artifact in the set because it reads the *actual* shipped internals:

- The drain/revoke-epoch/dedup machinery is **not** topology-agnostic — it's a 2-arm `match` over concrete runtime fields (`bump_revoke_epoch` is literally a Pool-only branch; Resident is a justified no-op). Opening the trait forces every author to re-implement the HikariCP-#1836 revoke fence correctly through a `Box<dyn Any>` guard and a no-op hook **they cannot express it through**.
- `AnyManagedResource` is **sealed** specifically so the framework can add methods without semver breakage; opening topology destroys that by construction.
- The cross-tenant `SlotIdentity` barrier is sound **only because one registry row owns one framework-held runtime**. An author topology with its own host-keyed cache re-opens the exact named cross-tenant-leak class **below** the structural barrier, invisibly.

**My adjudication for the design lead:** the domains and the adversary are not actually in conflict about *capability* — they're in conflict about *who implements it*. Every "needs open" case (#4 exclusive-stateful, #8 multiplexed, #10 SSH-channels, #17 disposable, #19 AMQP-channels) is satisfiable by **adding a small number of audited built-in topologies** rather than an open trait:

- `Bounded` (A1) → covers #7/#11/#12-excl/#17-cap/#22
- `Multiplexed` battery (N-sessions-over-one, framework-owned revoke fence) → covers #8-single-browser/#9/#10/#14/#19
- `ExclusiveStatefulOwner` (pinned, single-owner, stateful) → covers #4
- `Ephemeral` (fresh-per-acquire, no-reuse, no-share) → covers #17-instance

This gives authors the long tail of **resources** (open) on a short, audited list of **topologies** (closed) — which is the model already shipped, extended by 2–3 batteries. The genuinely-don't-fit cases (#12 lock-lease, #13 durable-wait, #16/#20 streaming) are **not topology shapes at all** — they're durability/streaming concerns that belong in the engine/daemon/storage tier (A7/A8/A9), and forcing them into an open *topology* trait is the wrong seam regardless.

**Net recommendation:** Keep topology **closed and framework-owned**. Ship `Bounded` (A1) for 1.0; ship `Multiplexed` + `ExclusiveStatefulOwner` + `Ephemeral` as additional audited built-ins (1.0 if the multiplexed/SSH/browser integrations are 1.0 targets, else 1.1). **Do not expose a downstream-implementable topology trait** — it re-opens cross-tenant bleed below the SlotIdentity barrier and hands authors a revoke-fence proof obligation they cannot satisfy through the erased seam.

---

# 1.0 vs 1.1 SUMMARY (for the design lead)

**MUST-HAVE-1.0** (correctness or enumerated-case-blocking):
- **A1** Bounded + runtime cap (unanimous, the one undisputed gap)
- **A2** Warming/Recovering/Tainted admission phases (12 cases) — **but CUT `Saturated`-as-phase**; saturation is the acquire error, not a gating gauge
- **A3** Optional author-supplied load surface **as diagnostics-only**; admission = **try-acquire-and-park on error taxonomy**, not read-and-decide (resolves the TOCTOU/bulkhead attack)
- **A4** Per-acquire session-init + **A5** fallible-async-release-with-reset-ordering + **A6** graceful-destroy-with-deadline (the "release/destroy is real async work, not Drop" cluster — 7+7+6 cases, collides with canon §11.4 which must be revised for server-side-stateful resources)
- **A10** Dedup affinity/anti-share modes (5 cases; framework-enforced, not author-discretion)
- **A11** Cost-aware semantic `check()` (7 cases)
- **A12** Parent-generation recovery (4 cases)
- New built-in topologies `Multiplexed`/`ExclusiveStatefulOwner`/`Ephemeral` **iff** their integrations are 1.0 targets

**DEFER-1.1** (no current consumer, or belongs to another tier):
- **A7** generic renew/fencing (scope only the lock-lease family to 1.0; generalize later)
- **A8** suspend/passivation across durable wait — **engine/storage tier**, not resource topology; 1.0 only ensures the guard *can* detach + carries a reattach key
- **A9** streaming handles — **possibly out-of-scope** (engine-daemon land); 1.0 only "don't forbid a guard outliving one call"
- **A13** first-class supervised background task
- **A14** open topology trait — **reject for 1.0**; closed + batteries instead

**Cross-cutting boundary the contract must state explicitly:** rate-limit/token-budget (#3/#5/#21 temporal budgets, #7-ffmpeg/#22 capacity) is **enforced in nebula-resilience, surfaced read-only by the topology** for the admission read. The topology exposes a phase the limiter *drives*; it does not own rate accounting. Getting this wrong duplicates the resilience crate inside resource (the R8-governor trap) and is the second TOCTOU/bulkhead breach the adversary names.

**The single sharpest finding:** the shipped model's biggest gap is **not** open-vs-closed topology (the adversary wins that — keep it closed). It is that **`release`/`destroy`/`check` are modeled as cheap/sync/Drop-shaped when 16 of 22 cases need them to be fallible, async, ordered, deadline-bound, and semantically aware** — and that **`ResourcePhase` is lifecycle when the engine needs an orthogonal admission axis fed by try-acquire outcomes, not a gauge**. Fix the lifecycle-async + admission-axis gaps and restore Bounded; defer durability/streaming/open-trait.
